commands.review_summary:
The PR moves queued system-event owner downgrades from prompt-visible `System (untrusted):` text into structured `forceSenderIsOwnerFalse` metadata across queue, reply, heartbeat, gateway, cron, agent, channel producer, scanner, test, and changelog surfaces.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection of current main shows `trusted: false` renders as `System (untrusted):`, reply runs infer owner downgrade from that rendered marker, and heartbeat derives `ForceSenderIsOwnerFalse` from queued `trusted === false` entries; no executable repro was run in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Protected `security` and `maintainer` labels plus owner-authority behavior make the next step explicit maintainer/security review, not cleanup close or an automated repair branch.
